quoted_tweet.text:
ShopifyがQ1決算を発表しました。

四半期GMVが1,000億ドル(約16兆円)を超えたのは2四半期連続。過去最高水準の決算でした。

なのに、株価は当日15%下落しました。

決算の中身、チェックすべきポイント、株価が下がった理由を整理します。↓

▼ 決算の中身

主要数字は全項目で市場予想を上回りました。

GMV(流通取引総額/Shopifyを使うお店全体での売上の合計):1,010億ドル(約16兆円、前年比+35%)

売上(Shopify自身が手数料・サブスクとして得た金額):31.7億ドル(約5,000億円、+34%)

売上総利益:15.5億ドル(約2,440億円、+32%)

営業利益:3.82億ドル(約600億円、+88%)

FCF(フリーキャッシュフロー/事業で稼いだ現金から投資を引いた、自由に使える現金):4.76億ドル(約750億円、マージン15%)

MRR(月次経常収益/サブスク料金の月額合計):2.12億ドル(約335億円、+16%)

4四半期連続で「30%超の成長」と「FCFマージン15〜19%」を両立しています。

特に伸びが目立つのがマーチャントソリューション売上の+39%で、Shopify Paymentsの浸透率は67%(前年比+3pt)、Shop PayのGMVは+59%、海外Shop Payに至っては+70%超の成長です。

AI関連の数字も注目に値します。

Sidekickの利用は前年比4倍、AI検索経由の注文は前年比13倍、Shopify Catalog経由でChatGPT・Copilot・Googleなどに表示される商品は10億点を超えています。

エンタープライズ側でも、Q1中に著名ブランドの新規導入が相次ぎました。

・BevMo!：米国西海岸を中心に展開する大手酒類ディスカウントチェーン
・Orvis：1856年創業の老舗アウトドア・釣り具ブランド
・Benetton Group：イタリア発のグローバルファッションブランド
・Victoria's Secret：米国最大手の女性向けランジェリーブランド
・Epic Shop(Vail Resorts)：北米大手スキーリゾート運営会社のEC事業
・Reitmans：カナダ最大級の女性向けアパレルチェーン

レガシーリテーラーがShopifyを基幹コマース基盤として選び始めている、という流れがはっきり見えます。

ファンダメンタルズとしては、文句のつけようがない四半期だったと言えます。

▼ チェックすべきポイント

決算を読むときに見ておきたい論点は、いくつかに整理できます。

①成長の質
Q1売上の約90%が、1年以上プラットフォームに在籍している既存マーチャントから来ています。リテンションの強さの裏返しでもあり、新規獲得の鈍化リスクの裏返しでもあります。

②AIの収益化
Sidekick利用4倍、AI経由注文13倍という数字は強いですが、絶対額がGMVのどの程度を占めるかは未開示です。今後ここが開示されると、新たな成長エンジンとして再評価される可能性があります。

③融資ビジネスの信用リスク
取引・ローン損失はQ1で1.16億ドル(約183億円、前年0.75億ドル=約118億円)と増加しています。マーチャント向け融資の与信が悪化していないか、次四半期も注視が必要です。

④海外比率
海外GMVは+45%成長、クロスボーダーは総GMVの16%。欧州7通貨でのマーチャント請求対応や、フランスでのcapital提供開始など、地味ですが地続きの拡張が続いています。

⑤関税・マクロ
クロスボーダー比率が高い分、デミニミス免税撤廃の影響を受けやすい構造です。https://t.co/mOGXquDHqyやmanaged marketsで対応していますが、政策変動は引き続きリスク要因です。

▼ なぜ株価が下がったのか

決算の中身は強いのに、当日の株価は107.85ドルまで-15.5%下落しました。理由は単一ではなく、複数の要因が重なっています。

最大の要因は、会社が示したQ2の業績見通しが減速していたことです。Shopifyは決算と同時にQ2の売上見通しを「高20%台」、つまり約27%成長と発表しました。Q1の34%から大きく減速する内容です。投資家は「34%が続く」前提で買っていたため、加速ストーリーが崩れた瞬間に売りが集中しました。

利益面の見通しも市場予想を下回りました。

EBITDAとGMVは予想を大きく上回ったものの、会社が示したQ2の売上見通しは市場予想線、営業利益の見通しは未達。Q2の営業費用は売上比35〜36%、株式報酬費用は1.45億ドル(約230億円)が見込まれており、利益率改善のペース鈍化への懸念が広がりました。

さらに、バリュエーションが完璧前提になっていたことも大きいです。フォワードPERは約85倍と高水準で、市場は「完璧な実行」を織り込んでいました。高PERグロース株の宿命で、「強いけど期待ほどではない」というだけで売られる構造になっていたわけです。

加えて、ローン損失の増加、株式投資の評価減によるGAAPベースの純損失、関税・マクロ不透明感、年初来で既に21%下落していた弱気センチメントが、それぞれ追加の売り材料として効きました。

要するに、「絶対値は素晴らしいが、市場が期待していた加速ではなく減速を示した」というのが本質です。

▼ まとめ

今回の決算は、ファンダメンタルズと株価の反応が大きく乖離した四半期でした。

ファンダメンタルズ側を素直に読むなら、GMV 1,000億ドル(約16兆円)超え、4四半期連続の高成長と高マージンの両立、AI関連の急速な普及、エンタープライズ獲得の継続と、AI時代のコマースインフラとしての優位性は強化されています。

株価側を素直に読むなら、Q2の業績見通しの減速、営業利益見通しの未達、高PERゆえの完璧期待、関税不透明感という、短期的な逆風が複数重なっています。

判断の分岐点はシンプルです。

構造的な勝者として見るなら、AI時代の販売チャネル(ChatGPT・Copilot・Google)を押さえ、20年分のコマースデータを持ち、エンタープライズ流入が続いている事実は、長期的にはむしろ強化されています。

短期的なリスクとして見るなら、成長減速・関税・高PERの三重苦が、次四半期以降も続く可能性があります。

次四半期(Q2実績)が、この乖離を埋める方向に動くかの試金石になります。会社見通し通りの27%台に着地するのか、それを上回って加速ストーリーが復活するのか。

「AI時代のコマースインフラ」という構造的なテーマと、「高PERグロース株の四半期ボラティリティ」という短期的な現象を、どこで線引きして見るか。

今回の決算は、Shopifyという銘柄の見方そのものを問い直す四半期だったと言えそうです。

kept.text:
一位中国创业者，靠着 Claude Code 搭建了13个AI代理，专门为独立站卖家打造Shopify店铺，仅凭一己之力，每月服务200位dropshipper，每人收费800美元。

他的工作台很简单，墙上挂着一块LG大屏，被分成3×2的网格，同时跑着6个Claude窗口。旁边还有一块竖屏，同样显示6个代理界面，再加上手边MacBook上的1个，13个AI代理同步开工，各司其职搭建店铺。

没有团队，没有主管，也没有任何后勤支持，只有他、几块显示器，以及每个窗口上不断跳动的API调用计数器。

他没有买任何订阅服务，全程按token计费。从第一位客户开始，这套13代理并行的模式就实现了自给自足。每完成一个店铺就能收入800美元，而13个窗口全天的成本加起来还不到80美元。

在第一个代理窗口里，他写下了一句系统提示，直接定义了AI的角色：“你是我的联合创始人兼工程师”。

这句话彻底解决了AI只是“助手”的问题。对他来说，Claude不再是简单的工具，而是他微型工厂里的正式员工，按工作量计费，从不摸鱼，也不用午休。

剩下的12个代理，他分别分配了不同任务，互不干扰、独立运行：
负责上架80款产品并重写文案；
针对客户细分领域设计首页布局；
配置购物车、支付接口和各国物流；
生成30套邮件营销序列用于冷启动；
设计50张banner和一套品牌LOGO；
搭建数据分析系统并做首页A/B测试。

放在普通设计公司，这些工作每项都要设计师或程序员花上两天，还要反复沟通、修改、开会。而他让13个代理同时并行，这边在写产品描述，那边已经在做首页，另一个窗口在出设计图，效率天差地别。

整个画面就像一座小型自动化工厂：13个Claude代理同步推进一个项目，而他只需要做两件事——把做好的店铺分给对应客户，以及决定下一单接谁。

从成本来看，这种模式远比养5人团队划算。他每天能产出6～7个完整店铺，每个售价800美元。传统机构做同样的店铺要收3500美元，还得耗时两周，而他全天API成本不到80美元。

桌面上线材不算整齐，显示器固定在支架上，没有正规办公室，也没有雇员，只有一张桌子、13个AI代理，以及每天排队发来订单的dropshipper。

在我眼里，这是今年见过效率最高的单人Shopify代建工厂，而且已经稳定跑通。当传统行业还在争论AI会不会取代设计师时，他已经用AI把生意做成了。

kept.text:
Shopify Flow に、ワークフローの版履歴機能が追加されました。編集・有効化・無効化・エクスポートといった操作が、担当スタッフ名とタイムスタンプ付きで自動記録され、ワークフロー詳細画面の「バージョン履歴」タブから確認できるようになっています。

複数人で運用しているストアほど、何か不具合が起きた時に原因特定に時間がかかる場面が多かったように思います。Flowはちょっとした条件変更でも結果が大きく変わるので、変更履歴が残らないこと自体が運用上のリスクでもありました。

不具合発生時に変更ポイントを辿る手がかりが残るようになったのは、運用面でかなり大きな安心材料だと感じています。Flowを本格的に作り込んでいるストアほど恩恵が大きいアップデートだと思います。

ただし、現時点ではいくつか制限もあるので、運用前に把握しておくとよさそうです。

- 過去バージョンへの復元（ロールバック）はできず、あくまで閲覧のみ
- 履歴の保持期間は1年間
- 一部の履歴データは2026年4月以降の分のみ
- 1バージョンに複数編集がある場合、ユーザーごとに最新7件まで表示
- Sidekick や Shopify 側の自動処理による変更も履歴に記録される
- Flow アプリにアクセスできるスタッフ全員が、すべてのワークフローの版履歴を閲覧可能

のようなやったらやっただけ得をする、良い意味でリスクとリターンが見合っていない魅力的なリスクというのはスモビジではそこら中に落ちているのですが、皆んな、

スモビジが面白いのは、参入障壁が「資金」でも「学歴」でもなく、こういった、「行動するかしないか」だけになっている領域がいくらでもあることです。

kept.text:
Shopify $SHOP 1Q26 Earnings
- Rev $3.2b +34% ↗️🟢 (vs 30-32% guide)
- GP $1.5b +32% ↗️🟢 margin 48.8% -76 bps ↘️🔴
- NG EBIT $523m +59% ↗️🟢 margin 16.5% +256 bps ✅
- EBIT $382m +88% ⤴️🟢 margin 12.1% +345 bps ✅
- NG Net Inc $471m +41% ↗️🟢 margin 14.9% +66 bps ✅
- Net Inc* -$581m ↘️🔴 margin -18.3% +1057 bps ✅
- OCF $481m +31% ↗️🟢 margin 15.2% -38 bps ↘️🔴
- FCF $476m +31% ↗️🟢 margin 15.0% -37 bps ↘️🔴

* includes a $1.1b and $1.0b unrealized loss in 1Q26 and 1Q25.

Biz Metrics
- GMV $100.7b +35% ↗️🟢
- US GMV $84.6b +33% ↗️🟢
- Int GMV $16.1b +45% ↗️🟢
- GPV $67.5b +41% ↗️🟢
- GPV/GMV penetration 67% +300bps ↗️🟢
- Shop Pay GPV $35b +59% ↗️🟢
- Offline GMV +33% ↗️🟢
- B2B GMV +80% ↗️🟢
- Shop App GMV +70% ↗️🟢
- MRR $212m +16% ↗️🟢

Subscription solutions
- Rev $750m +21% ↗️🟢
- Sub Rev - Standard $398m +11% ↗️🟡
- Sub Rev - Plus $210m +21% ↗️🟢
- Sub Rev - Apps, Themes, Domains, Variable Platform Fees $143m +64% ↗️🟢
- GP $602m +21% ↗️🟢 margin 80.3% +11 bps ✅

Merchant solutions
- Rev $2.4b +39% ↗️🟢
- GP $944m +40% ↗️🟢 margin 39.0% +39 bps ✅

Rev by Geography
- US $2.0b +33% ↗️🟢
- Canada $147m +24% ↗️🟢
- Europe $653m +42% ↗️🟢
- APAC $312m +30% ↗️🟢
- ROW $57m +39% ↗️🟢

Mgmt Guide
- Rev +28%+ ↗️🟢
- GP +25%+ ↗️🟢
- FCF margin 15%+ ✅

1 | Very strong quarter, broad-based across geographies, merchant sizes and channels, strong unit economics with cohort growth, even older merchants are growing.

Q1 reflects strength across all dimensions of our business, not just any one in isolation. Our growth is broad-based across geographies, merchant sizes and channels. International, enterprise, off-line and B2B are all scaling. Underneath all of this, the cohort dynamics continue to compound. I believe that remains one of the most underappreciated characteristics of our business. Each quarter's results are an aggregation of successes over many years of merchant cohorts. Each new cohort stacks on top of the prior one, and our newer cohorts are larger than the ones before them, a reflection of the breadth of merchants we are attracting. But what's incredible is that the older cohorts, even the merchants who have been on Shopify for many years, are not plateauing. They continue to grow.

2 | 90% of revenues are from merchants >1 year on Shopify’s platform.

As an example, in Q1, almost 90% of our revenue was from merchants who had been on the platform for more than a year. The driving force is our platform and product velocity. That's the structural advantage of Shopify. We give you everything you need by operating across the entire commerce stack. It's not the power of any one element of the platform

3 | Larger customers >$25m and $2-25m GMV are growing the fastest.

Diving deeper into different GMV perspectives, let's first look at merchant size. In recent quarterly calls, I've talked about 3 strata: merchants doing up to $2 million in GMV, those doing $2 million to $25 million in GMV and those doing more than $25 million. We saw strength across merchant GMV bands consistent with recent trends. The $2 million to $25 million GMV band added the most incremental revenues YoY, but the other 2 segments were not far behind, and the greater than 25 million band merchants are growing the fastest.

4 | Signed and went live with numerous big brands.

we're seeing more of the world's biggest brands migrating to us from all corners of commerce. In Q1, we signed with 3 legends of luxury, Mulberry, [ BevMo! ] and LVMH. And in fashion, we welcomed Rag & Bone, luxury outlets, The Outnet and Rue Gilt Groupe and the iconic Lands' End. BevMo!, one of the largest liquor store retailers in the U.S., has brought us in to power all of their locations with Shopify point of sale. And Orvis, the outdoor brand that was founded in 1856 is moving to Shopify for a full unified commerce solution. Meanwhile, Q1 saw us go live with incredible brands like The Benetton Group, Victoria's Secrets, Body, Epic Shop by Vail Resorts and Reitmans. And here's the best part. We're not just winning the retail legends of today, we're powering the retail legends of tomorrow, and it's happening really fast.

5 | Merchants >$100m GMV 2X in last 2 years, will see larger and larger brands.

In just the last 2 years, the total number of large merchants doing $100 million or more in GMV on Shopify has nearly doubled. That is real growth. And it's coming from merchants that are scaling into that category as well as those that are already there and looking to modernize for what's next. And all of this puts us in an incredibly strong position to continue driving this part of the business. On our last call, I said we'll see more billion-dollar brands born in the next 10 years than the last 100. And a lot of people thought that was hyperbole. It was not. Gruns is a perfect example, and everything we're building is designed to make this happen faster. So when I zoom out, this is what I see.

6 | Expect Shopify Payments penetration at 67% of GMV to continue to grow.

Merchant Solutions revenue grew 39%, driven primarily by the strength in GMV and increased penetration of Shopify Payments. $67 billion of GMV was processed on Shopify Payments in Q1. That's 41% higher than the prior year and 67% of GMV, 3 points higher than Q1 of 2025.

We see a clear path for the rate to continue moving higher, stemming from deeper penetration across all geographies, growing adoption in the 15 European countries in Mexico where we launched payments last year, expansion into new countries beyond the 39 where we are today and continued Shop Pay momentum.

7 | AI is now Shopify’s native language. AI now writes >50% of Shopify’s code, one of the highest amongst other companies

We are already in it. In 2026, AI is now Shopify's native language. We bet early on AI and forced its adoption. It's embedded in everything we do, the products we build, the channels we power, the way every single person on the team operates. AI has become an exoskeleton for everyone at Shopify, giving them a virtual team of agents and that makes room for rapid experimentation. It allows them to pursue multiple ideas at the same time and then double down on the winners.

I think actually, AI right now writes well over 50% of our code today, and that number is going up significantly, not down. But I think more than any other company, AI is Shopify's native language.

8 | AI is making entrepreneurship more accessible and more accelerated, and when there’s more entrepreneurship, Shopify wins as a platform for them.

AI is making entrepreneurship dramatically more accessible and in fact accelerated. That means we're going to see more entrepreneurs, and they're going to scale more easily. AI-powered shopping democratizes discovery. Reach is not just influenced by budget anymore, it is influenced by relevance, which benefits both merchant and buyer. And the right products find the right shopper at the right moment. And this is enormous potential for new and scaling merchants. And because we win when they win, it also has enormous potential for Shopify.

9 | AI is increasing complexity, not new, seen it before, Shopify will absorb complexity and become more valuable to merchants.

There's always going to be some market confusion when we see a significant shift like we're seeing right now with the rise of AI. We've seen it before. I'm sure we'll see it again. And every single time the world gets more complex, Shopify gets more valuable. We absorb more of that complexity into our systems and become more valuable to merchants.

10 | The Sidekick AI intelligent assistant saw rapidly growth in usage.

And Sidekick is the perfect example of this. As a reminder, this is our intelligent assistant, which is trained on our knowledge base, paired with completely personalized intel, it has about each merchant's particular business. Now last quarter, we told you the numbers were encouraging. Well, that was just the beginning. The number of weekly active shops using Sidekick in Q1 was up 4x year-over-year. We saw over 12,000 custom apps created in Q1 alone using Sidekick. And nearly half of all Shopify flows generated in Q1 were built with Sidekick. And theme edits just from last quarter are in the multimillions, growing over 1,000% in a single quarter. Every app built, every automation created, every task completed is the merchant

11 | Shopify brings speed, context, and simplified complexity behind checkouts, and gives merchants the ability to sell across every channel, every surface and every geography, and AI is allowing them to build more, ship more, and serve more merchants.

It's the speed, context and simplified complexity behind checkout. It's the ability to sell across every channel, every surface and every geography from day 1. Internally, we are making every function faster, sharper and more productive, and output per employee is improving through deliberate AI usage. The result is that we are building more, shipping more and serving more merchants. The leverage we have and continue to deliver is what funds ongoing investment in AI infrastructure, global reach and platform depth. That discipline is what we have demonstrated consistently. We will always lean into growth because as we grow, we invest more in driving success for our merchants and for Shopify.

12 | Shopify is the only platform the supports discovery and selling inside ChatGPT, Copilot and Google, all from one single system of record, dominating early in AI-driven traffic up 8X, orders from AI-powered searches up 13X.

So let's talk about these channels. We are the only platform that enables discovery and selling inside ChatGPT, Copilot and Google, all from one single system of record.
And the early signals on AI channels are really compelling. And in the first quarter, AI-driven traffic to Shopify stores has grown 8x year-over-year, while orders from AI-powered searches have increased nearly 13x. And within this, new buyer orders are occurring at nearly twice the rate of other channels.

13 | Where others are struggling with clean attributes, real-time pricing and accurate inventory, Shopify is able to do all that, traffic from catalog-powered AI searches are converting 2X more than general AI searches. Retail industry is noticing.

To date, we've structured more than 1 billion products with clean attributes, real-time pricing and accurate inventory so AI agents can surface the most relevant products in seconds, and the results speak for themselves. Traffic from catalog-powered AI searches converts 2x more than traffic from general AI searches where the agent is working from scraped or often outdated information from across the web. That is the value Shopify brings.

I mean Shopify catalog is now the authoritative source for AI product discovery. There's now 1 billion products across millions of merchants.The data is structured, the pricing is accurate. There's real-time inventory, clean attributes. And OpenAI and Microsoft are already using the catalog power discovery. But so I think generally, this plan, this agentic idea, agentic plan idea is working really well for us. And I think the retail industry has certainly taken notice.

14 | Merchants are running ad campaigns on Shopify, up 3X, and Shop campaigns are now  25% of their total GMV.

Campaigns, which is one of our ad products. Finding new customers is one of the hardest things we're running a business. Paid marketing has historically been expensive, complex and simply out of reach for smaller merchants who don't have the budget or the expertise to compete with larger brands. Well, campaigns is changing that. In Q1, the number of merchants with a live campaign was up 3x YoY.

That is not a small signal, that is a product that is starting to have a true impact on our merchants. And what I love is the impact this is having on SMBs, in particular, because a lot of them would not otherwise have had access to performance marketing at this level. For some of those smaller merchants, shop campaigns is contributing as much as 1/4 of their total GMV. That is not a nice to have. That is a growth engine.

15 | Shop App was extremely strong, GMV +70%, MAU +40%, unique buyers +50%, sign-in Shop usage up 3X.

Shop app had a strong Q1. GMV was up 70% YoY, a clear signal that the buyer network is deepening and Shop is becoming a meaningful commerce destination in its own right. Monthly active users grew over 40% YoY and unique buyers purchasing directly on Shop grew over 50% compared to Q1 of last year, meaning more new shoppers are discovering and buying through the Shop app than ever before. And remember, the Shop app is just one facet of Shop, which is the buyer-facing side of Shopify. Sign in with Shop is our user verification tool, which recognizes buyers across devices, stores and surfaces with no sign-in friction. And usage is growing steadily. We are up 3x YoY, and it is now enabled across nearly our entire merchant storefront base.

16 | Shopify co-developed Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) with Google, an open protocol that makes Agentic commerce work at scale, and with Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce, and Stripe all joining the council, it looks to become the universal protocol for commerce.

And the industry agrees, as you might have seen with the latest news on the Universal Commerce Protocol, or UCP, which we co-developed with Google. UCP is an open protocol that makes Agentic commerce work at scale. It enables the full commerce journey, product discovery, checkout, payment, post purchase across any platform with any payment processor. We co-developed UCP because we believe the future of commerce runs in open standards, not closed systems. And then we created the UCP Tech Council, the technical body that steers the protocol's direction to ensure it evolves to meet the needs of businesses, platforms, developers and consumers. We are now seeing the biggest and most innovative companies across essentially the entire industry coming together around UCP to help push Agentic commerce forward. And last month, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce and Stripe all joined the council, committing their expertise in Internet scale transaction processing to build one universal protocol for commerce. The companies that power how the world shops are now building on one standard, and Shopify is the center of how commerce gets done in the age of AI agents, and this is what it looks like in practice.

17 | Financial services is becoming more embedded and valuable as part of Shopify, not a standalone product, see a lot more to be done there.

if you think about financial services, the first product we've really had was capital, and that product is roughly 10 years old. That's something that we've had for a while, has worked really well. There's other suites of products that we provide in kind of financial services more broadly…And that's one of the things we found in our capital business where we've been really thoughtful in terms of how we do lending and to help them accelerate their business.

I think financial services is just becoming more embedded and a more valuable part of the Shopify platform. And it's not a stand-alone product. It's embedded in the platform that merchants already trust, and it's -- we think there's a lot more to do there

18 | Shopify’s GTM remains focused on velocity with SMBs and depth with enterprises.

Our go-to-market engine sort of runs 2 tracks in parallel. Obviously, SMB is all about velocity and the enterprise is really more about depth. And we've built this dedicated team and professional services that embeds into that enterprise motion. Product is unequivocally a major driver. When we show up, we show outcomes. We show speed, we show cost, we show conversion, we show simplicity. And I think more and more with these very large brands that are coming on, brands that I've mentioned on this call, they're looking for this unified commerce platform.

19 | Shopify remains focused on growing revenues and absolute gross profit dollars while delivering continued head count discipline, committing to strong FCF margins.

We continue to drive operating leverage through 2 key elements: growing gross profit dollars and delivering continued head count discipline. Both of these allow us to invest in further AI usage internally and our returns-based marketing, which in turn helps fuel more growth.

Gross profit has compounded at 29% annually over the past 3 years, and our commitment to these free cash flow margins remains unwavering. The business is durable, our position is unique, and our conviction is that the investments that we are making today in AI infrastructure in the merchant-facing services being built on top of it and in the data advantage that comes from powering a meaningful share of global commerce will further strengthen our position.

➡️ Final Takeaways Shopify $SHOP:

Shopify continues to do well in becoming the OS for commerce across all businesses and in the next phase of ecommerce, agentic commerce with UCP. Partnerships and large brands/enterprise, offline / POS, B2B, Cross-border, International, strategic advertising, Shop Pay, Shop App, branded checkout a,ll doing well. That’s the beauty of Shopify, optionality. And when customers do well, Shopify does well too.

kept.text:
🧵 Day 24/30 — #SystemDesign

REST vs GraphQL: How modern APIs deliver data differently
Frontend apps need data fast. But fetching too much or too little data creates performance problems.
That’s why API design matters.

Two popular approaches dominate modern backend systems:
→ REST → GraphQL
Both solve API communication differently.
 
⸻
 
REST API
REST exposes multiple endpoints.

Example:
→ /users/1 → /posts/1 → /comments/1
Simple and widely adopted.
Pros:
→ Easy to understand → Great caching support → Mature ecosystem → Works well for standard CRUD apps
But sometimes REST returns:
→ Too much data (over-fetching) → Too little data requiring multiple requests (under-fetching)
 
⸻
 
GraphQL
GraphQL exposes a single endpoint.
Client asks exactly what it needs.

Example:
{
  user {
    name
    posts {
      title
    }
  }
}

Pros:
→ Precise data fetching → Fewer network calls → Better frontend flexibility → Strong typed schema
Popular for complex frontend apps.
 
⸻
 
Real-World Usage
→ GitHub uses GraphQL APIs → Shopify heavily uses GraphQL → Most public CRUD APIs still use REST → Many companies use hybrid approaches
There’s no universal winner.
 
⸻
 
Key Tradeoff

REST: → Simpler backend architecture → Easier caching
GraphQL: → Flexible queries → More backend complexity
 
⸻
 
Hidden Challenges in GraphQL
→ Expensive nested queries → Harder caching → Query complexity attacks → Resolver performance issues
Powerful… but requires discipline.
 
#30DaysOfSystemDesign #GraphQL #BackendEngineering

quoted_tweet.text:
🧵 Day 23/30 — #SystemDesign

Pagination vs Infinite Scroll: Why loading everything breaks systems

Fetching all data at once feels simple…
until your API slows down, responses grow huge, and UX suffers.

Smart systems load data in chunks.

⸻

Pagination = page-based loading
Infinite Scroll = continuous loading

Both solve scale.
But serve different use cases.

⸻

When to use Pagination

→ Admin dashboards
→ Search results
→ Reports / tables

Users want control, navigation, and exact positions.

⸻

When to use Infinite Scroll

→ Social feeds
→ Content apps
→ E-commerce browsing

Users want seamless experience.

⸻

Key Backend Insight

Offset-based pagination:

→ Easy
→ Slower at large scale

Cursor-based pagination:

→ Faster
→ More scalable
→ Preferred in production

⸻

Tradeoff

Pagination:
→ Better control
→ SEO friendly

Infinite Scroll:
→ Better engagement
→ Harder state management

⸻

Golden Rule

Load less. Load fast.

#30DaysOfSystemDesign #Pagination #BackendEngineering

kept.text:
هذا الصيني بنى 13 وكيل AI داخل Claude Code لمتاجر Shopify، وصار يخدم لحاله أكثر من 200 شخص بالدروبشيبنق شهريًا، ويأخذ 800 دولار من كل واحد.

يجلس على مكتب واحد قدام شاشة LG معلّقة على الجدار، مقسّمة إلى 6 نوافذ Claude بنظام 3×2، وبجانبها شاشة عمودية فيها 6 نوافذ ثانية بنفس التقسيم، وفوق هذا كله فيه نافذة إضافية على الماك بوك اللي قدامه، يعني بالمجموع 13 Agent شغالين بنفس الوقت يبنون متاجر Shopify، وكل واحد ماسك جزء مختلف من المشروع.

لا فريق، لا مدراء، لا دعم فني، بس هو، الشاشة، وعدّاد الـ API اللي يتحرك فوق كل نافذة.

هو أصلًا مو مشترك باقة شهرية، بل يستخدم الدفع حسب استهلاك الـ API بالتوكنز، وحاسبها صح:
الـ13 Agent يغطّون تكلفتهم من أول عميل، لأن كل متجر ينباع بـ800 دولار، بينما كل النوافذ مع بعض تستهلك أقل من 80 دولار يوميًا.
في أول نافذة، حاط System Prompt قتل فكرة “المساعد” أو “الموظف” من الأساس:

"you are my new founder-engineer"

يعني النموذج فهم من أول لحظة على أي مستوى تم توظيفه:
مو عشان يعطي اقتراحات،
مو عشان “يساعد”،
مو عشان يكمل شغلك،
بل عشان يمتلك النتيجة كاملة.

بالنسبة لهذا الصيني، Claude ما عاد مجرد AI داخل IDE…
صار شريك داخل مصنعه الصغير، يتحاسب بالتوكن، وما يطلب بريك غداء.
أما الـ12 Agent الباقين، فموزعينهم على طبقات المتجر، وكل واحد داخل Context مستقل حتى ما يخرب على الثاني:
“ابنِ كتالوج فيه 80 منتج وأعد كتابة الوصف”
“صمّم الصفحة الرئيسية حسب نيتش العميل”
“اضبط السلة، الدفع، والشحن حسب الدولة”
“أنشئ 30 سلسلة إيميلات للتسويق”
“صمّم 50 بنر وشعار للبراند”
“فعّل التحليلات واختبارات A/B على الصفحة الرئيسية”
في وكالة تقليدية، كل مهمة مثل هذي تأخذ من مصمم أو مطور يومين كاملين:
يجمع Brief،
ينتظر التعديلات،
يدخل مكالمات،
ويراجع العميل…
بينما هذا الصيني عنده 13 Agent يشتغلون بالتوازي.
واحد يكتب الوصف،
الثاني يبني الصفحة الرئيسية،
الثالث يصمم البنرات،
وكل شيء يتحرك بنفس اللحظة.

وفي النهاية، الجدار قدامه شكله مثل مصنع:
13 روبوت Claude يكتبون داخل مشروع واحد،
وهو جالس على الكرسي قدامهم ما عنده إلا قرارين:
لمن يسلّم المتجر الجاهز،
ومن العميل اللي يدخل بعده.
غير كذا؟
ما يسوي شيء.

اقتصاديًا، الموضوع أرخص حتى من فريق صغير مكوّن من 5 موظفين.
مشغل واحد مثل هذا يقدر يقفل من 6 إلى 7 متاجر يوميًا بسعر 800 دولار للمتجر،
بينما وكالة تصميم تقليدية تطلب 3500 دولار لنفس المتجر وتأخذ أسبوعين كاملين عشان تسلمه،
وهو كل اللي يدفعه أقل من 80 دولار يوميًا على الـ13 نافذة كلها.

أسلاك طالعة،
شاشة مركّبة على Stand،
لا مكتب فاخر،
لا موظفين،
بس طاولة وحدة،
13 روبوت،
وطابور Dropshippers يرسلون طلبات جديدة كل صباح.
وبرأيي؟
هذا أكثر “مصنع Shopify فردي” كفاءة شفته هذه السنة،
وهو شغال الآن فعلًا،
بينما الوكالات التقليدية للحين جالسة تتناقش:
“هل الـAI راح يأخذ وظائف المصممين؟”
